c74object.name: dk.filter
c74object.digest: Apply filtering/routing to incoming descriptor messages
c74object.description: The dk.filter object allows you to selectively send incoming descriptor or spectralshape messages to one of two outlets depending on whether the filtering criteria is met. This allows you to fork processing based on audio characteristics.
c74object.discussion:
If you want to learn more about the underlying algorithm have a look at this article:
https://learn.flucoma.org/reference/datasetquery

inlet.description: Descriptor input as list or buffer~.
Descriptor input as list or buffer~ that will be filtered by the input of the left-most inlet.

outlet.digest: Descriptor output if conditions are met
outlet.description: Descriptor output if conditions are met. Output will be list or buffer~ based on what the input was.

outlet.digest: Descriptor output if conditions are not met
outlet.description: Descriptor output if conditions are not met. Output will be list or buffer~ based on what the input was.

attribute.name: filter
attribute.description:
Messages related to descriptor filtering.
After sending the filter message you send a series of additional arguments that determine what descriptor is being filtered, how it is being filtered, as well as further filtering strands combined with conditionals.
The available descriptors that can be used for filtering are:
loudness = loudness in dB
loudness_derivative = derivative of loudness in dB
centroid = centroid in MIDI pitch
centroid_derivative = derivative of centroid in MIDI pitch
flatness = spectral flatness in dB
flatness_derivative = derivative of spectral flatness in dB
pitch = pitch in MIDI pitch
pitch_confidence = pitch confidence in 0 to 1
The available spectralshape descriptors that can be used for filtering are:
centroid = centroid in MIDI pitch
spread = spectral spread in MIDI pitch
skewness = spectral skewness in ratio
kurtosis = spectral kurtosis ratio
rolloff_low = low spectral rolloff in MIDI pitch
rolloff_mid = mid spectral rolloff in MIDI pitch
rolloff_high = high spectral rolloff in MIDI pitch
flatness = spectral flatness in dB
crest = spectral crest in dB
You can filter by the following operators:
== = equals
!= = does not equal
< = less than
<= = less than or equal
> = greater than
>= = greater than or equal
You can join multiple queries using:
and
or
Therefore a complete message would look as follows:
filter loudness < -45 or filter centroid > 60 and flatness < -80
To reset the filtering you can send the following message: 'filter reset'
